flags.concern: Multi-drag config conversion: the Step 2 `applyMultiDragMoves` + `rowsToConfig` path is missing the required merge of `metaUpdates`/overlap patches into `meta` before serialization, so timed clips that get shifted by `resolveGroupOverlaps` would be rebuilt with stale `from`/`to` values.
flags.evidence: `applyMultiDragMoves()` returns both `nextRows` and `metaUpdates` after merging `resolveGroupOverlaps` patches (`src/tools/video-editor/lib/multi-drag-utils.ts:101-174`). `rowsToConfig()` serializes timed clips from `meta.from`/`meta.to`, not just the action bounds (`src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:192-267`). `resolveGroupOverlaps()` produces timing patches when a moved group is gap-fit (`src/tools/video-editor/lib/resolve-overlaps.ts:216-316`).

flags.concern: Drop-preview plumbing: Step 4 is incomplete for multi-drag new-track previews because `computeSecondaryGhosts` never sees a virtual target row today. While hovering a new-track zone, `useClipDrag` still falls back to `session.sourceRowId` when `trackId` is undefined, and `computeDropPosition` reports `screenCoords.rowTop` for the first/last existing row, not a virtual inserted row. Passing only an augmented row list will not place secondary ghosts in the new-track slot.
flags.evidence: During drag preview, `anchorTargetRowId` is `nextPosition.trackId ?? session.sourceRowId`, so `isNewTrack` keeps the anchor on the source row for ghost planning (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:299-314`). For new-track drops, `trackId` is intentionally undefined and `rowTop` is derived from `visualRowIndex` clamped to an existing row (`src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts:104-117,177-189`). `DropIndicator` also suppresses the normal ghost/line whenever `position.isNewTrack` is true (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/DropIndicator.tsx:94-130`).
